root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing/performance assumptions: under the race detector (or slow CI), the DDL owner can take longer to update, and the previous sleep/timeout budget was too small. This PR stabilizes it by extending the background job owner timeout (based on lease, capped) and sleeping long enough (based on lease) to ensure the owner change is observed deterministically.
fix_pattern: Increase time budget for async lease/owner checks under slow CI (e.g., extend timeout based on lease and sleep long enough to observe state change) to avoid race-mode timeouts.
